get.description:
List all saved versions for an agent in version-number order.

Return version metadata and configuration snapshots visible to the authenticated user. Return
403 for unauthorized access, 404 when the agent does not exist, and 500 on retrieval failures.

post.description:
Create a new version snapshot from the provided agent configuration.

Persist a versioned copy of the configuration and optional metadata so it can be activated or
compared later. Return 400 for invalid input, 403 for unauthorized access, and 404 when the
target agent does not exist.

get.description:
Get a specific agent version by version identifier.

Return the full version payload, including configuration and metadata, for authorized users.
Return 403 for unauthorized access, 404 when the version is not found, and 500 on read errors.

put.description:
Activate a specific version as the agent's current configuration.

Mark the selected version as active so future runs use its settings. Return 403 for
unauthorized access, 404 when the version is missing, and 400 for invalid activation requests.

get.description:
Compare two agent versions and return field-level configuration differences.

Load both versions, compute a structured diff, and return side-by-side version payloads for
review workflows. Return 403 for unauthorized access, 404 when either version is missing, and
500 if comparison fails.

post.description:
Rollback to a prior version by creating a new version from that historical snapshot.

Preserve version history by generating a fresh version entry instead of mutating old records.
Return 403 for unauthorized access, 404 when the source version is missing, and 500 on rollback
errors.

put.description:
Update editable version metadata without changing the stored configuration snapshot.

Modify details such as version name and change description while keeping configuration content
immutable. Return 403 for unauthorized access, 404 when the version is missing, and 500 for
update failures.

Update an existing agent with the provided configuration changes.

Apply partial updates to mutable agent fields while preserving access control checks for the
current user. Admins bypass metadata restrictions. Return 404 when the agent does not exist
and 403 when update restrictions apply.

delete.description:
Delete an agent owned by the current user.

Remove the agent when it is eligible for deletion and return the service result payload. Admins
bypass protection checks. Return 404 when the agent is missing, 403 for access denial, and 400
for protected agents.

get.description:
Get a single agent with its full persisted configuration.

Fetch the agent by identifier after user scoping checks and return the serialized response
model. Return 404 when the agent cannot be found.

List agents for the current user with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Parse optional query filters, including tool filters and content type, then return normalized
pagination metadata with agent results.

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: Sort field: name, created_at, updated_at, tools_count
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description: Sort field: name, created_at, updated_at, tools_count

parameters.name: has_mcp_tools

schema.description: Filter by agents with MCP tools
schema.title: Has Mcp Tools

parameters.description: Filter by agents with MCP tools

parameters.name: has_builtin_tools

schema.description: Filter by agents with builtin tools
schema.title: Has Builtin Tools

parameters.description: Filter by agents with builtin tools

parameters.name: tools

schema.description: Comma-separated list of tools to filter by
schema.title: Tools

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tools to filter by

parameters.name: content_type

schema.description: Content type filter: 'agents', 'templates', or None for agents only
schema.title: Content Type

parameters.description: Content type filter: 'agents', 'templates', or None for agents only

post.description:
Create a new agent for the current user.

Persist the requested agent configuration and return the created resource with default fields
populated by the service.

Generate an agent icon name and color palette from the provided metadata.

Use the agent name and optional description to derive a consistent icon set for UI display and
return the normalized icon fields.

Duplicate an existing agent with its current configuration. Admin only.

Create a new independent agent copying the source agent's name, description, icon, system
prompt, tools, and MCPs. The new agent starts at version 1 with is_default=false.

get.description: 查询某 Agent 已启用的技能列表及完整可用技能元数据。

put.description: 更新某 Agent 已启用的技能列表。

get.summary: Get River Pro builtin tool visibility
get.description: Return the River Pro builtin tool defaults used by future tasks.

200.description: Default builtin tool visibility for future River Pro tasks

put.summary: Update River Pro builtin tool visibility
put.description: Set which River Pro builtin tools are enabled by default for future tasks.

200.description: Updated builtin tool visibility for future River Pro tasks

post.description:
Start or resume an agent run for either a new task or an existing task.

Session identity is task identity: no `task_id` means start a new session,
while an existing `task_id` means continue that session even if its sandbox
later needs to be woken.

Accept multipart form input with optional file uploads and return run identifiers for
downstream polling or SSE streaming from the stream endpoint. Return 400 for invalid
new-task input, 404 for missing resources, and 402 for model, credit, or account limit
failures.

Retry a failed agent run by reviving it and re-enqueueing.

Only works on failed runs. Resets the run to pending and attempts enqueue again.
Return 404 if the run does not exist or cannot be retried.

get.description:
Stream SSE events for a task's active agent run.

Resolves the running agent run internally — the caller only needs the task ID.
Auth is checked before streaming starts, so errors return proper HTTP status codes.

parameters.name: token

schema.description: Authentication token for SSE stream access
schema.title: Token

parameters.description: Authentication token for SSE stream access

post.description:
Stop the currently running agent run for a task.

Resolves the running agent run internally — the caller only needs the task ID.

Generate an initial agent configuration from a natural-language chat description.

Use an LLM-backed setup flow to infer fields such as name, system prompt, tools, and icon
metadata, then return the structured configuration payload for the caller. Unhandled service
exceptions propagate as 500 errors.

get.description:
Discover available MCP tools for an agent from a remote MCP server.

Read MCP connection parameters from request headers, including required `X-MCP-URL`, then
return discovered tools without mutating agent configuration. Return 400 when required headers
are missing, 404 when the agent does not exist, and 500 for discovery failures.

post.description:
Enable or disable specific MCP tools for an agent at a given MCP server URL.

Persist tool-selection updates for the provided server transport and tool list while preserving
user ownership checks. Return 404 when the agent is missing and 500 for unexpected update failures.

put.description:
Replace all custom MCP server configurations for an agent in a single operation.

Treat the request MCP list as the new source of truth, then return the stored MCP definitions
after validation. Return 404 when the agent is missing and 500 when persistence fails.

get.description:
Get all tools configured for an agent, including builtin and MCP-backed tools.

Fetch the merged tool set visible to the current user for agent execution and UI management.
Return 404 when the agent does not exist, 403 for access denial, and 500 for retrieval errors.
List all projects for the authenticated user's account.

post.description: Create a new project.

get.description:
Get a specific project by ID with complete data.

Supports both authenticated and anonymous access (for public projects).

delete.description: Delete a project. Associated tasks keep data but lose project_id (SET NULL).

get.description: Get paginated list of tasks for the authenticated user.
Get task detail including sandbox and message count.

patch.description: Update task name, visibility, or project assignment.

delete.description: Delete task and all related data (sandbox, messages, agent runs).

post.description: Start or continue a task using a JSON chat payload and durable attachment references.

get.description: Get all messages for a task without role filtering, returning raw database content.

get.description: Get feedback. Can filter by task_id and/or message_id. Returns user's own feedback.
Get a specific feedback by ID (only if it belongs to the current user).
Get file upload configuration including size limits.

Fetch a remote file and upload to storage.

Create a new workflow app.

Args:
    payload: App creation data
    user_id: Authenticated user ID
    app_service: App service instance

Returns:
    Created app

get.description:
List workflow apps for the current user.

Args:

    name: Search apps by name (optional)
    page: Page number (1-based)
    page_size: Items per page (1-100)
    user_id: Authenticated user ID
    app_service: App service instance

Returns:

Update app metadata.

Args:

    payload: Update data
    user_id: Authenticated user ID
    app_service: App service instance

Returns:

Raises:

delete.description:
Delete app and all associated workflows.

Args:
    app_id: App ID
    user_id: Authenticated user ID
    app_service: App service instance

Returns:
    Success message

post.description:
Sign in with email and password to obtain an access token.

Returns a JWT access_token that can be used in the Authorization header
for subsequent API requests: `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`.

Uses a temporary Supabase client to avoid polluting the shared
singleton's auth state (sign_in_with_password mutates client headers).

get.description:
Get draft workflow for an app.

Returns None if draft doesn't exist yet.
Frontend should call sync endpoint to create it.

post.description:
Sync workflow draft (create if not exists, update if exists).

This endpoint uses lazy initialization - workflow draft is created
on first sync, not when app is created.

delete.description: Delete workflow draft for an app.

get.description:
Get the latest published workflow version.

Dify-aligned: returns null if no published version exists.

schema.title: Response Get Published Workflow Api Apps  App Id  Workflows Publish Get

post.description: Publish a workflow with optional version metadata.

Execute a single workflow node for debugging purposes.

Supported node types:
- start: Validates input variables and initializes workflow
- llm: Executes LLM call with prompt (future implementation)
- end: Collects workflow outputs (limited value in single-step mode)

Note: END and ANSWER nodes are executable in backend but hidden in frontend UI.

get.summary: Get last single-run result for a node
get.description:
Get the most recent single-step execution result for a node.

This endpoint retrieves historical execution data for the "Last Run" panel in the UI.

post.summary: Run workflow and stream events via SSE
post.description:
Run workflow and stream events via SSE.

Uses WorkflowAppGenerator + WorkflowAppRunner to execute in a worker thread and
stream queue events back to the client.

Run the latest published workflow.

Aligned with Dify's POST /workflows/run endpoint.

Supports response_mode:
- "blocking": Wait for completion, return final result (JSON)
- "streaming" or None: Return SSE stream (default)

post.summary: Run specific workflow version by ID (Dify-aligned)
post.description:
Run a specific published workflow version by ID.

Aligned with Dify's POST /workflows/{workflow_id}/run endpoint.

Validates:
- UUID format
- Not a draft version (use /draft/run instead)

get.description:
Get workflow run history list.
Returns recent runs sorted by created_at desc.

schema.title: Response Get Workflow Run History Api Apps  App Id  Workflow Runs Get

get.description: Get workflow run detail including graph snapshot.

get.summary: Get node executions for a workflow run
get.description:
Get all node executions (tracing data) for a workflow run.
Returns execution details for each node that ran.

schema.title: Response Get Workflow Run Node Executions Api Apps  App Id  Workflow Runs  Workflow Run Id  Node Executions Get

post.description:
Stop a running workflow execution.

Aligned with Dify dual-channel stop mechanism:
- Channel A: Redis stop flag (stops output/SSE)
- Channel B: Command channel abort (stops GraphEngine)

Always returns success - fire-and-forget pattern.
If task already completed, the commands are simply ignored.
Admin stop endpoint using no_user_check stop flag.

get.summary: Download workflow draft variable full content

get.description:
Get system variables for workflow in Variable Inspect format.

System variables include: query, files, user_id, workflow_id, workflow_run_id.
Like Dify's GET /apps/{app_id}/workflows/draft/system-variables endpoint.

Returns:
    {"items": [...VarInInspect objects...]}

get.summary: Get all draft variables for a workflow
get.description:
Get all cached draft variables for a workflow in Variable Inspect format.

These variables are saved during single-node debugging and used to provide
upstream context for subsequent node executions.

Returns format compatible with Variable Inspect UI.

delete.summary: Clear all draft variables for a workflow
delete.description:
Clear all cached draft variables for a workflow.

Use this when starting a fresh debugging session or when the workflow
structure has changed significantly.

get.summary: Get draft variables for a specific node
get.description:
Get cached draft variables for a specific node in Variable Inspect format.

Useful for inspecting what outputs were cached from a node execution.

delete.summary: Clear draft variables for a specific node
delete.description:
Clear cached draft variables for a specific node.

Use this when you want to re-run a specific node without using
its previously cached outputs.

get.summary: Get single draft variable with value
get.description:
Get a single draft variable by ID (with value).

Like Dify's GET /apps/<app_id>/workflows/draft/variables/<variable_id>

patch.summary: Update draft variable name or value

delete.description:
Delete a single draft variable.

Like Dify's DELETE /apps/<app_id>/workflows/draft/variables/<variable_id>

put.summary: Reset variable to last execution value
put.description:
Reset variable to last execution value.

Like Dify's PUT /apps/<app_id>/workflows/draft/variables/<variable_id>/reset

Returns:
- 200 with variable if reset successful
- 204 if variable was deleted (no execution record)

get.summary: Get environment variables from workflow definition
get.description:
Get environment variables defined in workflow.

Like Dify's GET /apps/<app_id>/workflows/draft/environment-variables

Returns variables from workflow.environment_variables JSON field.

get.summary: Get default configurations for all workflow node types
get.description:
Get default configurations for all workflow node types.

Returns a dictionary mapping node type (START, END, LLM, etc.) to
their default configuration. Frontend uses this to initialize new nodes.

No authentication required - this is public configuration data.

get.summary: Get default configuration for a specific workflow node type
get.description:
Get default configuration for a specific workflow node type.

Supports query parameters for nodes with language-specific defaults:
- CODE node: ?code_language=python3 or ?code_language=javascript

Returns:
    {"type": "code", "config": {...}}

Like Dify's GET /apps/<app_id>/workflows/default-workflow-block-configs/<block_type>

parameters.name: code_language

schema.description: Code language for CODE node (python3 or javascript)
schema.title: Code Language

parameters.description: Code language for CODE node (python3 or javascript)

get.description:
List published workflow versions with pagination.

Like Dify's GET /apps/<app_id>/workflows endpoint.

Query params:
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- limit: Items per page (default: 10, max: 100)
- user_id: Filter by creator ID
- named_only: Only show versions with marked_name (default: false)
Get a specific workflow version by version string (e.g., 'v1', 'v2').

patch.description:
Update version metadata (marked_name, marked_comment).

Like Dify's PATCH /apps/<app_id>/workflows/<workflow_id> endpoint.

delete.description:
Delete a workflow version.

Constraints:
- Cannot delete draft version
- Cannot delete the latest published version
- Cannot delete if tool_published=true

Like Dify's DELETE /apps/<app_id>/workflows/<workflow_id> endpoint.

post.description:
Restore a published version to draft.

Copies the selected version's graph, features, and environment_variables
to the draft version.

Like Dify's restore workflow functionality in version history panel.

post.description:
API endpoint for account initialization (fallback/retry).
Most users will be initialized automatically via webhook on signup.

Webhook endpoint called by Supabase database trigger when a new user is created.

This webhook is triggered automatically by a database trigger (trigger_welcome_email)
when a new row is inserted into auth.users table. The trigger makes an HTTP POST
request to this endpoint using pg_net.

This webhook automatically:
1. Initializes account (free tier subscription + River agent)
2. Sends welcome email

All initialization happens automatically on the backend, eliminating
the need for client-side initialization calls.

TaskSandboxResponse.title: TaskSandboxResponse
TaskSandboxResponse.description:
Task sandbox metadata for the web app.

`live_view_url` is the read-only noVNC viewer URL (port 6080) used by the
browser tool view to render the agent's live Chrome window inside an
iframe. It is `None` until the agent's first `sb_browser_tool` call has
opted into Daytona's Computer Use display stack AND the desktop ports are
listening; the web app polls this endpoint until the field flips
non-null, then renders the iframe.

`live_view_width` / `live_view_height` carry the canonical pixel
dimensions of that live view (i.e. Chrome's window size inside the
sandbox) so the web app can lock its iframe container to the same aspect
ratio. Both are populated alongside `live_view_url`; both are `None` when
the live view itself is not yet available.

- "blocking": Wait for completion, return final result
- "streaming": Return SSE stream (default if None)
